title: Chrysaspis barbulata
taxonomy: Chrysaspis barbulata is classified within the family Rutaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the citrus or rue family. The genus Chrysaspis is part of the tribe Boronieae. Within the family Rutaceae, Chrysaspis barbulata is considered to be a rare and endemic species found in a specific region. The classification of Chrysaspis barbulata is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic analysis. Further research is necessary to confirm the exact classification and relationships within the Rutaceae family.
uses: Chrysaspis barbulata has been traditionally used in medicine, primarily in the Himalayan region. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
